title: The Cherry Trees by Edward Thomas — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
THE cherry trees bend over and are shedding
On the old road where all that passed are dead,
Their petals, strewing the grass as for a wedding
This early May morn when there is none to wed.
meaning: "The Cherry Trees" by Edward Thomas is a deep poem consisting of 4 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "THE cherry trees bend over and are shedding On the old road where all that passed are dead,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
